requiredFindings.summary: The patch blocks only protocol-relative URLs and still permits http:// or https:// absolute URLs.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: A caller-controlled absolute URL can send the internal Authorization header to an attacker or cause SSRF.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Parse the final URL and reject any origin outside the configured baseURL, or disable absolute URLs for this client.

requiredFindings.summary: Tests cover //evil.test but not https://evil.test or http://evil.test.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: The vulnerable path is still untested.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add tests for absolute scheme URLs, mixed-case schemes, encoded values, and header leakage.
